name: Nightbane — Basic Skill Package
description: Default R.C.C. skill package for Nightbane who were normal young adults or students at the Becoming — typical Dark Day campaign characters with high-school or early college backgrounds.
packageNotes: Default package for normal young adults at the Becoming — high-school student or college freshman with little adventuring training (p. 89). Typical Dark Day campaign characters.
Shared Nightbane R.C.C. rules (pp. 88–89): skill packages are guidelines — G.M. may allow related skills outside groupings for convincing backgrounds; not obligated to do so.
If the character later joins a Faction, the G.M. may allow picks from other faction skill packages as they gain levels (p. 89).
Secondary skills (p. 89): six at creation, plus two at levels 4 and 8 — shared by all core Nightbane skill packages. No O.C.C. percentage bonus; category access matches Related Skills.
Related skills: ten at creation; +2 at level 2, +2 at level 5, +1 at level 8, +1 at level 12 — all new skills start at 1st-level proficiency (p. 89).

Facade Hand to Hand must be purchased as a Related skill (Basic = 1 slot, Expert = 2, Martial Arts or Assassin = 3). Morphus has innate Hand to Hand: Martial Arts on `race_nightbane` (+1 APM and Morphus combat bonuses).
Related W.P. selections are limited to civilian weapons (no military arms such as assault rifles or heavy weapons).
Starting cash 3D6×100 (p. 90); faction members may receive additional mission funds. Typical civilian gear — car/motorcycle, clothing, personal effects (see startingEquipment).

name: Guardian R.C.C. Skills
description: Shadow O.C.C. for the Guardian R.C.C. — optional player protectors of light (pp. 189–192). Memory loss leaves 10 related + 8 secondary skills from a simple, mostly non-technological list (all start at base level). +1 related at levels 3, 6, 9, and 12. Typically Hand to Hand: Basic (included). P.P.E. powers of light and nullify magic on `race_guardian`; master psionics via `ispEngine`. Cannot learn magic.
packageNotes: Shadow O.C.C. for `race_guardian` (auto-mounted via forcedOccId). Optional player R.C.C. — supernatural beings of light; section pp. 189–192.
Origin: voluntary transformation with faded memories; resolve to protect humankind. Book leaves creator/goals unanswered — G.M. tentatives encouraged (pp. 189–190).
Alignment: almost always Principled or Scrupulous — evil Guardians are a hunted minority sensed by others (`race_guardian` demographics) (pp. 191–192).
Memory loss — no fixed O.C.C. core skills. Select 10 related and 8 secondary from the restricted list below; all new skills start at base level (p. 192).
Level-up related skills: +1 each at levels 3, 6, 9, and 12 (`levelUpSkillChoices`) (p. 192).

Category limits (p. 192): Communications basic radio only; Domestic any +10%; Medical First Aid/Holistic Medicine/Paramedic +10%; Pilot automobile/motorcycle/horsemanship +5%; Technical any +5%; Wilderness any +5%; Physical and W.P. any; Electrical, Espionage, Mechanical, Military, Rogue, Science, Pilot Related forbidden.
Typically Hand to Hand: Basic included (`handToHandRules.defaultSkillId: hth_basic`). Upgrade paths use related slots for Expert (2) or Martial Arts (3) (pp. 191–192).
Combat as per Hand to Hand selected; supernatural strength damage on `race_guardian` (pp. 191–192).
P.P.E.: P.E.×5 + 20/level — powers of light, nullify magic, hover/fly on race row (`ppeEngine`) (pp. 191–192).
Psionics: master psionic tier — Empathy, Mind Block, Presence Sense, See Aura at start; +1 Sensitive or Healing power per level after 1st (`ispEngine`) (pp. 191–192).
Magic: none — cannot learn or cast; nullify magic and innate light powers only (`race_guardian` occLimitations) (pp. 191–192).
Natural abilities (precognition, senses, regeneration, solar sustenance, light deprivation, alliances) live on `race_guardian` — see `classAbilities` cross-refs (pp. 190–192).
Starting resources: street clothing and 2D4×10 cash; allies may raise funds to 2D6×100 (usually necessities/helping others) — `finances`/`startingEquipment` (p. 192).
Guardians rarely carry weapons — prefer natural abilities over firearms (p. 192).
Experience: typical P.C.s 1st–2nd level; Elder NPCs average 1D6+6 — Nightbane & Guardian XP table (`nightbane_core_nightbane_guardian`, p. 233 index) (pp. 191–192).

leyLine.notes: Nightbane RPG pp. 67–68 — master innate psionics inherit psionic_global_rules.json (×1.5 range/duration near ley line within 2 mi; ×2 at nexus within 1 mi; psionic energy damage ×2 at nexus only).

meditation.notes: Nightbane RPG pp. 67–68 — inherits psionic_global_rules.json (6 I.S.P./hour meditation; 2 I.S.P./hour sleep or total relaxation; meditation costs no I.S.P.).

psychicApm.notes: Nightbane RPG pp. 68, 191–192 — combat varies with Hand to Hand skill; psionic attacks per melee equal physical hand-to-hand attacks when psionic powers are active.

classAbilities.name: Optional Player R.C.C.
classAbilities.description: Supernatural protectors of light — optional P.C.s with even less remembered origin than Nightbane. Social reactions range from worship to panic (pp. 189–190).

classAbilities.name: Origin & Mystery
classAbilities.description: Outcasts serving a higher power or alien force — voluntary transformation, faded memories, drive to help humankind. Book does not answer who created them or whether they can be corrupted (pp. 189–190).

classAbilities.name: Alignment
classAbilities.description: Almost always Principled or Scrupulous. Evil Guardians are a tiny hunted minority — other Guardians sense and seek to destroy or capture them (`race_guardian` demographics) (pp. 191–192).

classAbilities.name: Appearance & Exposure
classAbilities.description: ~5 ft albino-like features; always trapped in alien shape unlike Nightbane Facade. Disguise via wigs, makeup, contacts; See Truth reveals aura of invisible light — metadata on race row (pp. 189–190).

classAbilities.name: Non-Lethal Ethos
classAbilities.description: Prefer subtle, non-lethal resolution; kill humans only when innocents are at stake or target is truly evil. Destroy evil supernaturals (diabolic Nightbane, Nightlord minions, true vampires) out of hand when necessary (pp. 189–190).

classAbilities.name: Limited Skill Memory
classAbilities.description: 10 related + 8 secondary from restricted non-technological list — all start at base level due to memory loss (`occRelatedSkills` / `secondarySkills`) (pp. 189, 192).

classAbilities.name: Related Skill Growth
classAbilities.description: +1 related skill at levels 3, 6, 9, and 12 — same category limits (`levelUpSkillChoices`) (p. 192).

classAbilities.name: Hand to Hand Program
classAbilities.description: Typically Hand to Hand: Basic included at creation. Expert (2 slots) or Martial Arts (3 slots) via related upgrade paths — `handToHandRules` (pp. 191–192).

classAbilities.name: Supernatural Strength Damage
classAbilities.description: Hand-to-hand damage uses supernatural strength table — combat as per Hand to Hand selected (`race_guardian` strengthCategory) (pp. 191–192).

classAbilities.name: Combat Bonuses & Saves
classAbilities.description: +1 initiative, +3 Perception, +1 dodge, +2 roll with impact, +4 pull punch, +4 save vs magic, +3 save vs psionics, +5 save vs horror factor — `race_guardian` innateBonuses.modifiers (pp. 191–192).

classAbilities.name: Precognitive Senses
classAbilities.description: Instinctive gut feelings toward places help is needed — rarely clear; G.M. should not overuse (adventure openings, brief flashes) — cross-ref race classAbilities (pp. 190–191).

classAbilities.name: Supernatural Senses
classAbilities.description: Nightvision 500 ft; all light spectrums; see in blinding light; see invisible and recognize illusions on Perception 10+ — metadata on race row (pp. 191).

classAbilities.name: Super-Regeneration & Healing Touch
classAbilities.description: Passive 2D6 healing/hour. Healing Touch: 1 P.P.E./point healed (max P.E. per touch), 2 actions, once per minute, light aura — `race_guardian` innateBonuses.activation (pp. 191).

classAbilities.name: Powers of Light
classAbilities.description: Energy blasts 1D6 S.D.C./P.P.E. (+3 strike); shields 5 S.D.C./P.P.E. (1 minute/level); body illumination as sunlight at no P.P.E. (half effect vs sun-hating entities) — metadata (pp. 191).

classAbilities.name: Nullify Magic
classAbilities.description: Spend P.P.E. exceeding spell cost to counteract (flash of light; 1 action/attempt). Must guess P.P.E. before knowing spell cost — metadata on race row (pp. 191).

classAbilities.name: Hover & Flight
classAbilities.description: Hover 2 P.P.E. (2 minutes/level); fly 6 P.P.E. (2 minutes/level, max 40 mph / 2,000 ft) — metadata (pp. 191).

classAbilities.name: Solar Sustenance & Light Deprivation
classAbilities.description: Solar powered — no food/water/air; few hours daily light required. Deprivation rules and Nightlands penalty on `race_guardian` metadata (pp. 191).

classAbilities.name: P.P.E.
classAbilities.description: P.E.×5 + 20 per level — fuels light powers and Healing Touch (`ppeEngine`; race vitals metadata) (pp. 191–192).

classAbilities.name: Master Psionics
classAbilities.description: Master psionic (save 10+). Start Empathy, Mind Block, Presence Sense, See Aura; +1 Sensitive or Healing power each level after 1st — `ispEngine` (pp. 191–192).

classAbilities.name: I.S.P.
classAbilities.description: M.E.+1D6×10 at 1st level; +10 per level — `ispEngine` / race psionics metadata (pp. 191–192).

classAbilities.name: No Magic
classAbilities.description: Cannot learn or manipulate magic — nullify magic and innate light powers only (`occLimitations.forbiddenCategories`: Magic) (pp. 191–192).

classAbilities.name: Alliances & Hangouts
classAbilities.description: Work with humans, psychics, magic users, and Nightbane enemies of the Ba'al; good Wampyrs may join. Hangouts: soup kitchens, churches, cities, slums, wilderness retreats — metadata on race row (pp. 189, 192).

classAbilities.name: Equipment & Finances
classAbilities.description: Rarely armed — dislike guns. Start street clothes and 2D4×10 cash; allies may provide up to 2D6×100 — `finances`/`startingEquipment` (p. 192).

classAbilities.name: Experience
classAbilities.description: Typical P.C.s 1st–2nd level; Elder NPCs average 1D6+6 — Nightbane & Guardian XP table (`nightbane_core_nightbane_guardian`, p. 233 index) (pp. 191–192).

miscellaneous: One set of street clothing (p. 192)
Optional disguise kit: wigs, hair dye, makeup, colored contact lenses (pp. 189–190)

name: Wampyr R.C.C. Skills
description: Shadow O.C.C. for the Wampyr R.C.C. — optional player undead (01–02% slow-kill aberration; pp. 188–189). Retains free will, soul, and pre-transformation memories: 10 related + 8 secondary skills at 1st level (no fixed category list — GM approval). Hand to Hand purchased from related slots; combat as per H2H with supernatural strength damage. +1 related skill at levels 4, 7, 10, and 12. Nine vampire psionics (no Super-Hypnotic) plus one additional power per level after 1st. Not merged with `nightbane_true_vampire`.
packageNotes: Shadow O.C.C. for `race_wampyr` (auto-mounted via forcedOccId). Optional player R.C.C. — slow-kill aberration (01–02% from `race_secondary_vampire` creators); section pp. 188–189.
Origins: slow-kill victims whose vampiric domination failed — retain free will and a soul. Theories: pure soul, strong will, or latent Nightbane heritage (`race_wampyr` metadata) (pp. 188–189).
Excluded from `nightbane_true_vampire` module — Wampyr mechanics live on `race_wampyr` + this shadow O.C.C.; not Master/Secondary/Wild tier undead (pp. 188–189).
Alignment: any except Principled. Scrupulous Wampyrs avoid drinking blood from unwilling victims. Available as a player character (pp. 188–189).
R.C.C. skills (p. 189): select 10 related and 8 secondary skills at 1st level reflecting occupation/education before transformation — unlike true vampires, Wampyrs retain past-life memories.

The book provides no fixed related/secondary category list — only slot counts. Catalog uses open category access (all `any`) so the picker is not artificially restricted; narrative fit requires Game Master approval.
Level-up skills (p. 189): +1 related skill each at levels 4, 7, 10, and 12 (`levelUpSkillChoices`).

Hand to Hand must be purchased as a Related skill (Basic = 1 slot, Expert = 2, Martial Arts or Assassin = 3; Assassin requires evil alignment). Combat as per Hand to Hand selected; damage uses supernatural strength table on `race_wampyr` (pp. 188–189).

Psionics: start with all vampire psionic powers except Super-Hypnotic Suggestion; select one additional power from any psychic category every level after the first — `ispEngine` (I.S.P. M.E.+2D4×10, +8/level) (pp. 188–189).
Magic: none — any magic known before transformation is lost forever (`race_wampyr` occLimitations.forbiddenCategories: Magic) (p. 189).
Natural abilities (regeneration, sunlight, blood dependency, anti-taboo immunities, vampire lore, alliances) live on `race_wampyr` — see `classAbilities` cross-refs (pp. 188–189).
Weapons and equipment vary with occupation and situation (p. 189).
Starting resources: 2D4×100 cash and 2D6×1000 in property (vehicles and equipment) — `finances` (p. 189).
Experience: varies for NPCs; uses Wampyr XP table (`nightbane_core_wampyr`, p. 233 index) (p. 189).

meditation.notes: Nightbane RPG pp. 67–68 — inherits psionic_global_rules.json (6 I.S.P./hour meditation; 2 I.S.P./hour sleep or total relaxation; meditation costs no I.S.P.).

classAbilities.description: Slow-kill aberration (01–02% from `race_secondary_vampire` creators) — retains free will and a soul when vampiric domination fails. Available as P.C.s (Principled excluded) (pp. 188–189).

classAbilities.name: Origin Theories
classAbilities.description: Pure soul, strong will, or latent Nightbane heritage may explain failed domination — vampire intelligences do not know the truth (`race_wampyr` metadata) (pp. 188–189).

classAbilities.name: Alignment & Scrupulous Restraint
classAbilities.description: Any alignment except Principled. Scrupulous Wampyrs avoid drinking blood from unwilling people — metadata.scrupulousAvoidsUnwillingBlood on race row (pp. 188–189).

classAbilities.name: Excluded True-Vampire Module
classAbilities.description: Not merged with `nightbane_true_vampire` — lacks mist/bat/wolf metamorphosis, telepathic minion link, and true-vampire invulnerability. `metadata.excludedFromTrueVampirePowersModule` on `race_wampyr` (pp. 188–189).

classAbilities.name: Super-Regeneration & Mortality
classAbilities.description: Heal 2D6 S.D.C./Hit Points every melee round — supernatural strength and endurance. Unlike true vampires, reduced below zero and can die from conventional attacks (guns, knives) — metadata.diesBelowZeroHitPoints (pp. 188–189).

classAbilities.name: Half Damage from Metal Weapons
classAbilities.description: Metal weapons inflict half damage — no other normal vampire invulnerabilities. Staking damages heavily but does not incapacitate — metadata on race row (pp. 188–189).

classAbilities.name: Pre-Transformation Skill Package
classAbilities.description: 10 related + 8 secondary skills at 1st level reflecting former occupation/education — past-life memories retained (`occRelatedSkills` / `secondarySkills`) (p. 189).

classAbilities.name: Open Skill Categories (GM Approval)
classAbilities.description: Book provides no fixed category whitelist — only slot counts. All categories `any` in catalog; picks must fit pre-transformation life with Game Master approval (p. 189; ingest precedent).

classAbilities.name: Related Skill Growth
classAbilities.description: +1 related skill at levels 4, 7, 10, and 12 — same open pool and GM-fit rule (`levelUpSkillChoices`) (p. 189).

classAbilities.name: Hand to Hand Program
classAbilities.description: Hand to Hand purchased from related slots (Basic 1 / Expert 2 / Martial Arts or Assassin 3; Assassin evil-only). Combat as per Hand to Hand selected — `handToHandRules` (pp. 188–189).

classAbilities.name: Supernatural Strength Damage
classAbilities.description: Hand-to-hand and melee damage uses the supernatural strength table — `race_wampyr` strengthCategory (p. 189).

classAbilities.name: Vampire Psionics (No Super-Hypnotic)
classAbilities.description: Starts with all vampire psionic powers except Super-Hypnotic Suggestion — nine granted catalog powers via `ispEngine.grantedAbilityIds`; see race metadata.psionicGrantIdsExcludingSuperHypnotic (pp. 188–189).

classAbilities.name: Per-Level Psionic Growth
classAbilities.description: Select one additional psionic power from any psychic category every level after the first — `ispEngine.perLevelSelection` (pp. 188–189).

classAbilities.name: I.S.P.
classAbilities.description: M.E.+2D4×10 at 1st level; +8 per level of experience — `ispEngine` / race psionics metadata (pp. 188–189).

classAbilities.name: Combat Bonuses & Saves
classAbilities.description: +1 initiative, +3 Perception, +5 save vs horror factor, +5 save vs mind control and possession, immune to vampire mind control — modifiers and metadata on `race_wampyr` (pp. 188–189).

classAbilities.name: Blood Dependency
classAbilities.description: Must drink one pint of blood every 72 hours. Starvation frenzy begins after the first week without blood; three days in frenzy to reach full feeding frenzy — cross-ref Vampire Powers section (pp. 188–189).

classAbilities.name: Vulnerability to Sunlight
classAbilities.description: Direct sunlight: 2D6 damage per minute; shade allows regeneration to heal. Daytime shade/underground penalties apply even without direct sun — `race_wampyr` innateBonuses.activation (pp. 188–189).

classAbilities.name: Anti-Vampire Immunities
classAbilities.description: Running water, holy symbols, herbs of protection, and native-soil sleep requirements do not affect Wampyrs — metadata on race row (pp. 188–189).

classAbilities.name: Instinctive Vampire Lore
classAbilities.description: Knows all strengths and weaknesses of true vampires, including those that do not apply to Wampyrs — effective vampire hunters; metadata.instinctiveTrueVampireLore (pp. 188–189).

classAbilities.name: No Magic
classAbilities.description: Cannot work magic; pre-transformation spell knowledge lost forever — `occLimitations.forbiddenCategories`: Magic (p. 189).

classAbilities.name: Alliances & Enemies
classAbilities.description: Hunted on sight by vampire intelligences and `race_master_vampire`. May ally with vampire hunters, Nightbane, rebellious secondaries, or Nocturnes (Iosef Pendragon). Evil/anarchist may serve Nightlords to hunt true vampires. Guardians avoid but rarely destroy; good Wampyrs may join Guardian groups — metadata on race row (pp. 188–189).

classAbilities.name: Equipment & Finances
classAbilities.description: Weapons and gear vary with occupation. Start 2D4×100 cash and 2D6×1000 property — `finances` / optional `startingEquipment` (p. 189).

classAbilities.name: Experience
classAbilities.description: Experience level varies for NPCs — Wampyr XP table (`nightbane_core_wampyr`, p. 233 index) (p. 189).

miscellaneous: Weapons and equipment vary with pre-transformation occupation and current situation (p. 189)
Optional GM issue: vampire-hunter kit, street gear, vehicle/property from 2D6×1000 starting assets

name: Doppleganger R.C.C. Skills
description: Shadow O.C.C. for the Doppleganger R.C.C. — R.C.C. skills same as normal humans (p. 160). For player characters, determine the Earth double's O.C.C. to see which skills and categories are available; the Doppleganger page provides no fixed category list — consult the Game Master. Random or NPC doubles: skills may be rolled randomly or assigned at GM discretion; mirroring a known individual uses 1D6 per skill (1–2 unknown, 3–4 at −10% of the double's level, 5–6 at the same level). Hand to Hand purchased from related slots (same as humans). Combat uses the Supernatural Strength damage table on `race_doppleganger`.
packageNotes: Shadow O.C.C. for `race_doppleganger` (auto-mounted via forcedOccId). All player characters are awakened (pp. 159–160).
R.C.C. skills same as normal humans — determine the Earth double's O.C.C. to see what skills are available (pp. 160–161).

No fixed related/secondary category list on the Doppleganger page. Random doubles: skills determined randomly or as the Game Master sees fit (p. 159). Catalog uses open category access (all `any`) with provisional 10 related + 8 secondary so the picker is not artificially restricted — narrative fit and category limits follow the mirrored human O.C.C. at the table.
Specific double: identical attributes to the counterpart; roll 1D6 per skill — 1–2 unknown, 3–4 at −10% of the double's level, 5–6 at the same level (pp. 159–160).
Alignment (specific double): percentile table on p. 160 — same, one category down/up, total opposite, or random shift (see `classAbilities`).

Hand to Hand must be purchased as a Related skill (Basic = 1 slot, Expert = 2, Martial Arts or Assassin = 3; Assassin requires evil alignment). Combat as humans; hand-to-hand damage uses Supernatural Strength table on `race_doppleganger` (pp. 160–161).

Awakened minor psionics: 40% of non-psychic doubles gain two minor powers from one category (Physical, Healing, or Sensitive) — `ispEngine` roadmap; psychic doubles use all counterpart powers at two-thirds I.S.P. (pp. 160–161).
Magician doubles only may use magic (two-thirds counterpart P.P.E. on race row). Supernatural abilities (Earth survival, regeneration, vulnerabilities) live on `race_doppleganger`.

Experience: varies as humans; if the double dies, starts at mirror image's level and may advance beyond the double when more active (p. 161). Opposing Nightlords marks the character a traitor — torture before death if captured (p. 159).

leyLine.notes: Nightbane RPG pp. 67–68 — when awakened minor psionics apply, inherits psionic_global_rules.json (×1.5 range/duration near ley line within 2 mi; ×2 at nexus within 1 mi; psionic energy damage ×2 at nexus only).

meditation.notes: Nightbane RPG pp. 67–68 — inherits psionic_global_rules.json (6 I.S.P./hour meditation; 2 I.S.P./hour sleep or total relaxation; meditation costs no I.S.P.).

psychicApm.notes: Nightbane RPG p. 68 — psionic attacks per melee equal physical hand-to-hand attacks when psionic powers are active.

classAbilities.name: Counterpart O.C.C. Skills (GM approval)
classAbilities.description: R.C.C. skills same as normal humans — no fixed category list on the Doppleganger page. Determine the Earth double's O.C.C. for available skills and category limits; consult the Game Master before finalizing related and secondary picks (pp. 160–161).

classAbilities.name: Random Double Creation
classAbilities.description: Random Dopplegangers are rolled per the book table; skills determined randomly or as the Game Master sees fit (p. 159). Catalog open access is provisional until mirrored-O.C.C. workflow enforces counterpart limits.

classAbilities.name: Specific Double Skill Mirror
classAbilities.description: When mirroring a known individual, use identical attributes; roll 1D6 per skill — 1–2 unknown, 3–4 at −10% of the double's level, 5–6 at the same level (pp. 159–160). Encoded on `race_doppleganger` innateBonuses.metadata.

classAbilities.name: Alignment Mirror (specific double)
classAbilities.description: 01–20% same alignment; 21–50% one category down; 51–70% one category up; 71–80% total opposite (good/selfish→evil, evil→good); 81–00% random shift — roll 1D6 counting right from the double's alignment (p. 160).

classAbilities.name: Awakening & Earth Urge
classAbilities.description: Most Dopplegangers live in a daze until Nightlord psionic torture awakens them — filled with urge to reach Earth. Spontaneous awakening (double's death backlash, dimensional sweep to Earth with 48-hour deadline, etc.) also possible. Not all awakened doubles must murder their counterpart (pp. 159–160).

classAbilities.name: Earth Double Survival (48 hours)
classAbilities.description: On Earth without resolution, dies within 48 hours unless the human double is killed, one party returns to the Nightlands, or both are on opposite worlds simultaneously. Permanent Earth life requires the double's death. Good-aligned visitors who resist murder still perish after 48 hours — see `race_doppleganger` (pp. 159–160).

classAbilities.name: Minor Psionic Awakening (40%)
classAbilities.description: After awakening, 40% of non-psychic doubles gain minor psionics — select two powers from one category (Physical, Healing, or Sensitive) via `ispEngine` roadmap. Before awakening, no psionic powers (pp. 160–161).

classAbilities.name: Psychic Counterpart Powers
classAbilities.description: Dopplegangers of psychic humans gain all counterpart psionic powers at two-thirds I.S.P. (M.E. + 2D6 when rolled independently) — see `race_doppleganger` psionics block (pp. 160–161).

classAbilities.name: Magician Counterpart Magic
classAbilities.description: Only Dopplegangers of human magicians can use magic; all others are mundane. Magician doubles have two-thirds of counterpart P.P.E. — average Doppleganger P.P.E. otherwise 1D4 (pp. 160–161).

classAbilities.name: Supernatural Hand-to-Hand Damage
classAbilities.description: Combat bonuses and Hand to Hand selections same as humans; hand-to-hand damage uses the Supernatural Strength table (`race_doppleganger` strengthCategory / innateBonuses.metadata.handToHandDamageTable) (pp. 160–161).

classAbilities.name: Awakened Horror Save
classAbilities.description: +2 to save vs horror factor when true nature is revealed — awakened Dopplegangers only (all player characters); encoded on `race_doppleganger` innateBonuses (p. 161).

classAbilities.name: Facade Hand to Hand
classAbilities.description: Hand to Hand must be purchased as a related skill (Basic = 1 slot, Expert = 2, Martial Arts or Assassin = 3; Assassin requires evil alignment). Combat otherwise as humans (pp. 159–161).

classAbilities.name: Experience & Advancement
classAbilities.description: Experience level varies as humans. If the human double dies, the Doppleganger starts at the mirror image's level and may advance normally; awakened characters can exceed the double's level when more active (p. 161). No fixed O.C.C. level-up skill roadmap — follow mirrored human O.C.C. when applicable.

classAbilities.name: Alliances
classAbilities.description: Servants of Nightlords work under Hounds, Hound Masters, Night Princes, Hollow Men, and cultists. Resistors often ally with Nightbane, vampires, humans, and Guardians (pp. 159, 161).

classAbilities.name: No Nightbane Double
classAbilities.description: Nightbane never have Dopplegangers — indicates Nightbane are a separate species (`race_doppleganger` innateBonuses.metadata.nightbaneNeverHasDoppleganger) (pp. 160–161).

name: Hound R.C.C. Skills
description: Shadow O.C.C. for the Hound R.C.C. — minimal fixed skill package (pp. 162–163): natural tracking 80%, understand (not speak) all languages magically, swimming and climbing 98%, W.P. Polearm and W.P. Sword. All skills live on the race row; no related or secondary picks. Hand to Hand: Assassin at 1st level plus one extra attack per melee (natural abilities on `race_hound`). NPC minion — not player characters (Diabolic predators). No magic or psionic powers.
packageNotes: Shadow O.C.C. for `race_hound` (auto-mounted via forcedOccId). NPC stat block — evil supernatural predators; cannot be player characters (pp. 162–163).
Alignment: Diabolic (always) (p. 163).
Nightlord shock troops — magical constructs resembling black-metal skeletal warriors; hunt for pain and fear. Since Dark Day, thousands walk Earth (pp. 161–162).
R.C.C. skills are minimal and fixed on the race row — no related or secondary skill picks at creation or level-up (p. 163).
Fixed package (p. 163): natural tracking 80%; understand (but not speak) all languages magically; swimming and climbing 98%; W.P. Polearm and W.P. Sword.
Combat: equivalent to Hand to Hand: Assassin with +1 attack per melee starting at 1st level — encoded on `race_hound` innateBonuses (`hth_assassin` default here) (pp. 162–163).

No magic or psionic powers (p. 163).
Typical starting experience: 1D4+1 (`nightbane_core_hound_hunter` XP table); almost none progress beyond 5th level (p. 163).

Primary weapon: Darkblade Spear (3D6 + P.S.; cuts through all known materials). Serve Nightlords; sometimes commanded by Dopplegangers or Hollow Men; work alongside Hunters (`race_hunter`); led by Hound Masters (`race_hound_master`) (pp. 162–163).

classAbilities.name: Fixed Minimal R.C.C. Package
classAbilities.description: All R.C.C. skills granted on `race_hound` innateSkills — tracking 80%, understand-all-languages (not speak), swimming/climbing 98%, W.P. Polearm, W.P. Sword. No elective related or secondary slots (p. 163).

classAbilities.name: Languages (Understand Only)
classAbilities.description: Magically understands all languages but cannot speak — unlike Hound Masters, who can speak all languages magically (pp. 162–163).

classAbilities.name: NPC Only (Diabolic Predator)
classAbilities.description: Evil supernatural predators — alignment Diabolic always; not recommended as player characters (pp. 162–163).

classAbilities.name: Shock Troop Role
classAbilities.description: Nightlord magical constructs — ultimate warriors and trackers who live for hunt and slaughter. Lowliest Nightlord commands 100+; since Dark Day thousands patrol Earth. Darkblade spears cut tanks open like sardine cans (pp. 161–162).

classAbilities.name: Hand to Hand: Assassin (+1 APM)
classAbilities.description: Combat equivalent to Hand to Hand: Assassin with one additional strike/attack per melee from 1st level — `handToHandRules.defaultSkillId` + `race_hound` innateBonuses.modifiers.apm (pp. 162–163).

classAbilities.name: Supernatural Tracking
classAbilities.description: Smell and track supernatural creatures within 60 ft (18.3 m); base 50% + 5% per level. Tracks Nightbane Morphus signatures — very hard to shake once acquired. Encoded on `race_hound` (pp. 162–163).

classAbilities.name: Illusion & Concealment Immunity
classAbilities.description: Immune to illusions and concealment — sees through Shroud Talent, invisibility, and magical/psionic disguise. Cross-ref `race_hound` classAbilities (p. 163).

classAbilities.name: Self-Healing Trance
classAbilities.description: Heals all damage if allowed to stand still for one full hour (regenerative trance) — `race_hound` innateBonuses.metadata.selfHealTranceHours (p. 163).

classAbilities.name: Partial Invulnerability & Armor Rating
classAbilities.description: Half damage from non-magical weapons/attacks; A.R. 13 vs normal weapons, A.R. 0 vs magical/supernatural attacks. Metallic exoskeleton — no worn body armor (pp. 162–163).

classAbilities.name: Magic Vulnerability
classAbilities.description: Double damage from magic artifacts, magic weapons, Nightbane Talents, and powers/spells — `race_hound` innateBonuses.metadata (p. 163).

classAbilities.name: Darkblade Spears
classAbilities.description: Primary weapon — 3D6 plus P.S. damage; cuts through all known materials including steel. Rarely issued other weapons (pp. 162–163).

classAbilities.name: Alliances
classAbilities.description: Serve Nightlords and their avatars; sometimes placed under Dopplegangers or Hollow Men. Typically work alongside Hunters (`race_hunter`); large bodies led by Hound Masters (`race_hound_master`) (p. 163).

classAbilities.name: Experience Limits
classAbilities.description: Typical level 1D4+1; almost no Hound progresses beyond 5th level — `race_hound` demographics / XP table `nightbane_core_hound_hunter` (p. 163).

miscellaneous: Darkblade Spear (3D6 + P.S.; cuts through all known materials including steel)
Metallic exoskeleton — no separate body armor (pp. 162–163)

name: Ashmedai R.C.C. Skills
description: Shadow O.C.C. for the Ashmedai R.C.C. — eight related skills from any category (+10% and I.Q. bonus; prerequisites required) plus twelve secondary skills (pp. 165–166). Supernatural instinctive combat on `race_ashmedai`; cannot learn Hand to Hand skills. Physical-skill combat bonuses apply only in human or humanoid shape. Innate minor psionics: Alter Aura, Empathy, Mind Block, See Aura, Telepathy. I.S.P. 2D4×10+20 (no per-level gain). NPC minion — typically Diabolic or Miscreant; a few Aberrant. Uses Psychic-equivalent XP table.
packageNotes: Shadow O.C.C. for `race_ashmedai` (auto-mounted via forcedOccId). NPC stat block — Crawling Horrors; Nightlord special agents and independent P.P.E. predators (pp. 165–166).
Alignment: typically Diabolic or Miscreant; a few Aberrant — good and neutral alignments excluded on race row (pp. 165–166).
Master shape-shifters (mouse to elephant; not insects) used for espionage, NSB "Men in Black" infiltration, and assassination; renegades hunt humans on Earth (pp. 165–166).
Select 8 related skills from any category (+10% O.C.C. bonus plus I.Q. bonus). Prerequisites cannot be ignored — grey unavailable picks per Radical Visibility (p. 166).
Select 12 secondary skills at creation — no O.C.C. percentage bonus on secondary picks unless a specific skill grants otherwise (p. 166).
Combat bonuses from Physical skills apply only while in a human or humanoid shape — supernatural instinctive combat and racial bonuses otherwise (`race_ashmedai` innateBonuses) (pp. 165–166).
Cannot learn any Hand to Hand combat skills (`hth_none`; empty upgrade paths). May still select Weapon Proficiencies via related/secondary slots (p. 166).
Innate minor psionics at 1st level: Alter Aura, Empathy, Mind Block, See Aura, Telepathy (`ispEngine.grantedAbilityIds`). No magic powers; no Nightbane Talents (pp. 165–166).
I.S.P.: 2D4×10+20 at 1st level — no book per-level I.S.P. gain (`perLevelFormula: 0`). Minor psychic saving throw class (pp. 165–166).
Shape-shifting, P.P.E. predation, bio-regeneration, supernatural vision, and combat modifiers live on `race_ashmedai` — see `classAbilities` cross-refs (pp. 165–166).
Elite agents may carry Nightbane's Skin artifact (<20 exist) and standard NSB Federal-agent kit — see `startingEquipment` (pp. 165–166).
Typical starting experience: 1D6+1 (`nightbane_core_ashmedai_psychic_sorcerer` XP table — Psychic equivalent). Average Nightlord commands 5–10 Ashmedai among minions (p. 175 cross-ref).

meditation.notes: Nightbane RPG pp. 67–68 — inherits psionic_global_rules.json (6 I.S.P./hour meditation; 2 I.S.P./hour sleep or total relaxation; meditation costs no I.S.P.).

classAbilities.name: Elective R.C.C. Skill Package
classAbilities.description: Eight related skills from any category (+10% O.C.C. bonus plus I.Q. bonus; prerequisites required) and twelve secondary skills at creation — no O.C.C. % on secondary picks (p. 166).

classAbilities.name: Prerequisites Required
classAbilities.description: Related-skill prerequisites cannot be waived — unavailable picks greyed with explanation per Radical Visibility (p. 166).

classAbilities.name: NPC Only (Crawling Horror)
classAbilities.description: Worm-like Nightlord agents and independent predators — not player characters. Used as special agents, infiltrators, and assassins (pp. 165–166).

classAbilities.name: Alignment
classAbilities.description: Typically Diabolic or Miscreant; a few Aberrant. Good and neutral alignments excluded — `race_ashmedai` demographics (pp. 165–166).

classAbilities.name: Shape-Shifting

S.D.C. penalties by size; H.P. constant across shapes; individual human mimicry 25% base. Cross-ref `race_ashmedai` classAbilities and innateBonuses.activation (pp. 165–166).

classAbilities.name: P.P.E. Predation
classAbilities.description: Feeds on victims' life force (P.P.E.), not flesh — terror and agony "flavor" the energy; victim's P.P.E. doubles at moment of death. Cross-ref `race_ashmedai` (pp. 165–166).

classAbilities.name: Supernatural Vision
classAbilities.description: No eyes in true form — sees via supernatural senses through any shape. See invisible and in total darkness up to 2000 ft (610 m) — `race_ashmedai` innateBonuses.metadata (p. 166).

classAbilities.name: Bio-Regeneration

name: Sorcerer O.C.C.
description: Bookish arcane scholar — meticulous study of formulas, rituals, and incantations. Sorcerers sense ley lines and nexus points, may learn spells of any level through study (Pursuit of Magic), and wield wizard magic with strong saves versus the supernatural.
packageNotes: Civilized arcane tradition — learned wizardry rooted in ancient Egypt, Greece, and Persia; modern recruits from colleges and universities (history, archaeology, occult sciences) (pp. 115–116).
Attribute requirements: I.Q. 12, M.E. 10, P.E. 12 (p. 116).

Initial spell knowledge: three spells from each of levels 1–4 (twelve total); thereafter one new spell per level equal to character level (pp. 116–117). Parenthetical "two from each" on p. 120 treated as book errata — see ingest precedents.
Pursuit of Magic: additional spells and rituals of any magic level may be learned or purchased at any time regardless of experience level (p. 116; Magic Section p. 121+).
P.P.E.: 1D6×10 + P.E. at 1st level; +2D6 per additional level — living battery of mystic energy (p. 116).
Magic bonuses: +4 save vs magic, +4 vs possession, +2 vs mind control, +4 vs horror factor (p. 116).
Related skills: ten at creation (two must be Science); +2 at level 3, +1 at level 6, +1 at level 9, +1 at level 12. Military and Rogue unavailable (pp. 116–117).
Hand to Hand must be purchased as a Related skill (Basic = 1 slot, Expert = 2, Martial Arts or Assassin = 3; Assassin requires evil alignment) (p. 116).
Secondary skills: six at creation — no O.C.C. percentage bonus; category access matches Related Skills (p. 117).
Starting cash 3D6×100 + 200; property/assets 3D6×1,000 (equipment, vehicles) (p. 117).

classAbilities.description: Know and understand rituals, symbols, incantations, and paraphernalia — distinguish authentic occult materials from forgeries, determine general purpose of items, and identify supernatural forces involved. name: Master Vampire R.C.C. Skills
description: Shadow O.C.C. for the Master Vampire R.C.C. — willing first-generation undead who accepted a vampire intelligence's essence (pp. 179–181; NPC villain; not recommended as P.C.). Retains pre-transformation skills frozen at rebirth proficiency (O.C.C. bonuses forgotten; I.Q. bonus only). +3 new skills per 100 years of unlife at 2nd-level proficiency (frozen). Six natural attacks per melee, master psionics, and shared true-vampire powers on `race_master_vampire`. Optional frozen pre-rebirth magic. Typical experience 1D6+1 (2nd–7th level).
packageNotes: Shadow O.C.C. for `race_master_vampire` (auto-mounted via forcedOccId). First-generation undead — only masters choose undeath willingly; NPC villain, not recommended as player characters (pp. 179–181).
Alignment: any evil — radiates supernatural evil. Principled, Scrupulous, Unprincipled, and Anarchist excluded on race row (pp. 179–181).
Vampire intelligence origin: alien essence fragment; every undead created strengthens the intelligence's anchor toward dimensional manifestation (pp. 179–180).

Pre-rebirth skills (p. 181): mirror whatever skills the living character had — proficiencies frozen at transformation level and never increase. O.C.C. skill bonuses forgotten; only I.Q. bonus (if any) applies. No fixed slot count — record former-life skills at the table.

Century skills (p. 181): +3 additional skills per 100 years as a vampire at 2nd-level proficiency (frozen, never increase). Limited to Communications, Domestic, Pilot, Technical, Rogue, Wilderness, and Weapon Proficiencies — `occRelatedSkills` categoryRules (century pool only).
No secondary skill picks in the book (p. 181). No Hand to Hand program — six natural attacks per melee on race row (`hth_none`) (pp. 180–181).
Pre-rebirth magic (optional, p. 181): if the character knew magic before undeath, retain that knowledge frozen at rebirth level — never learn new spells or increase P.P.E. (1D6×10 on race vitals).

Psionics: master psionic tier — `ispEngine` grants nine catalog powers at 4th-level potency plus Super-Hypnotic Suggestion (vampire-exclusive). Mind control over other vampires at no I.S.P. (shared module). I.S.P. 3D6×10, frozen per level (pp. 180–181).

Shared undead suite: `nightbane_true_vampire` module (pp. 183–188) — blood hunger, bite/slow kill, metamorphosis, summoning, invulnerability, regeneration, etc. Master-only telepathic link and two human slave cap on race metadata.
Equipment: most disdain weapons/technology; enslaved servants may use cars, guns, and modern conveniences (pp. 180–181).
Typical starting experience: 1D6+1 (`nightbane_core_night_prince_vampire` XP table — shared with Night Prince and other vampire R.C.C.s) (pp. 180–181).

leyLine.notes: Nightbane RPG pp. 67–68 — master innate psionics inherit psionic_global_rules.json (×1.5 range/duration near ley line within 2 mi; ×2 at nexus within 1 mi; psionic energy damage ×2 at nexus only).

meditation.notes: Nightbane RPG pp. 67–68 — inherits psionic_global_rules.json (6 I.S.P./hour meditation; 2 I.S.P./hour sleep or total relaxation; meditation costs no I.S.P.).

psychicApm.notes: Nightbane RPG pp. 180–181 — up to two psionic attacks per melee; each psionic attack counts as ~2 hand-to-hand actions (two psionic + two physical possible). Overrides default 1:1 psychic APM link.

classAbilities.name: NPC Only (Willing Undead)

classAbilities.name: Alignment & Aura
classAbilities.description: Any evil alignment — radiates supernatural evil. Good and selfish alignments excluded (`race_master_vampire` demographics) (pp. 179–181).

classAbilities.name: Vampire Intelligence & Origin
classAbilities.description: Alien intelligences send essence fragments across the Megaverse; the first willing soul accepts undeath. Every vampire the master creates strengthens the intelligence's anchor — at thousands of undead, the intelligence may enter the world through a dimensional Rift (pp. 179–180).

classAbilities.name: Hit Point Equivalent
classAbilities.description: Uses 3D6×10 Hit Point equivalent (not normal S.D.C./H.P.) — nearly impervious to conventional weapons. Shared rules in `nightbane_true_vampire` module (pp. 183–188) (pp. 180–181).

classAbilities.name: True Vampire Powers Module
classAbilities.description: Blood hunger, bite/slow kill, metamorphosis, summoning, limited invulnerability, super-regeneration, and faction interactions — `metadata.trueVampirePowersModuleId: nightbane_true_vampire` merged onto `race_master_vampire` (pp. 183–188 cross-ref; tier lore pp. 179–181).

classAbilities.name: Century Skill Gains

classAbilities.description: Supernatural strength damage; killing bite 3D6 + P.S. bonus (damages other vampires) — `race_master_vampire` metadata (pp. 180–181).

classAbilities.name: Psionic Combat Mix
classAbilities.description: Combine up to two psionic attacks with physical attacks per melee — each psionic attack counts as ~2 hand-to-hand actions (two psionic + two physical possible). See `supernaturalRuleOverrides.psychicApm` (pp. 180–181).

classAbilities.name: Combat Bonuses & Immunities
classAbilities.description: +3 initiative, +4 Perception, +5 save vs horror factor. Impervious to mind control, psionic/magic sleep, and paralysis — metadata + modifiers (pp. 180–181).

classAbilities.name: Master Psionics

classAbilities.name: Super-Hypnotic Suggestion
classAbilities.description: Vampire-exclusive (p. 186): 20 I.S.P., line-of-sight eye contact (eyes glow red/yellow), 5 minutes per level, standard save; victim obeys commands except those utterly against alignment — not in psionics catalog.

classAbilities.description: Automatic dominance over other vampires at no I.S.P. — shared module; attacker's eyes glow, voice like thunder; target saves vs psionic attack or obeys totally for 2D6 hours (pp. 183–188 cross-ref).

classAbilities.name: Undead Hierarchy

classAbilities.name: Equipment Preference
classAbilities.description: Most vampires disdain weapons and technology; enslaved servants may use cars, guns, and other modern conveniences while the master relies on natural powers (pp. 180–181).

classAbilities.name: Faction Notes
classAbilities.description: Fears Guardians (never deals; flee or destroy). Loathes Nightlords as rivals for Earth. Sees Nightbane as dangerous rivals but may strike temporary alliances of convenience — metadata on race row (pp. 179–180).

classAbilities.description: Typical NPC level 1D6+1 (2nd–7th) — Night Prince & Vampire XP table (`nightbane_core_night_prince_vampire`, p. 233 index) (pp. 180–181).

name: Secondary Vampire R.C.C. Skills
description: Shadow O.C.C. for the Secondary Vampire R.C.C. — optional player undead (slow-kill victims of `race_master_vampire`; pp. 181–182). Ten retained skills at 1st-level proficiency (frozen; never increase) from a limited category list. +2 additional skills at levels 3, 6, 9, 11, and 13 (same categories; frozen at 1st level). Five natural attacks per melee; major psionics and shared true-vampire powers on `race_secondary_vampire`. Optional frozen pre-rebirth magic (half of remembered spells). P.C.s start at 1st level; typical NPC experience 1D6+1 (2nd–7th).

Alignment: selfish or evil — radiates supernatural evil. P.C.s fighting blood hunger may be Unprincipled (highest good possible); Principled and Scrupulous excluded. Anarchist and Aberrant evil more common than Unprincipled (pp. 181–182).
Origin: slow-kill victim of `race_master_vampire` — retains more human memories than master or wild undead; may sometimes defy the vampire intelligence (pp. 181–182).
Retained skills (p. 182): select ten related skills from allowed categories — all start at 1st-level proficiency and never increase.
Level-up skills (p. 182): +2 additional related skills at levels 3, 6, 9, 11, and 13 — same category limits; frozen at 1st-level proficiency (`levelUpSkillChoices`).
Allowed categories only: Communications, Domestic, Pilot, Technical, Rogue, Wilderness, and Weapon Proficiencies — `occRelatedSkills` categoryRules.
No secondary skill picks in the book (p. 182). No Hand to Hand program — five natural attacks per melee on race row (`hth_none`) (pp. 181–182).

Pre-rebirth magic (optional, p. 182): if the character knew magic before undeath, retain about half of remembered spells at frozen power levels — player selects which half. No new spells; P.P.E. frozen at 1D4×10 on race vitals.
Psionics: major psionic tier via `ispEngine` (I.S.P. 2D6×10). Susceptible to `race_master_vampire` mind control — not impervious to all domination (pp. 181–182).

Slow-kill creation by a secondary vampire is inconsistent — roll 01–02% Wampyr, 03–44% secondary, 45–00% wild (~58% wild per book prose); see `race_secondary_vampire` innateBonuses.activation (pp. 181–182; module pp. 183–188).
Shared undead suite: `nightbane_true_vampire` module (pp. 183–188). Secondary tier: one human vampire slave cap; no master telepathic link.
Equipment: prefer natural powers; at most may drive a car or very rarely use a gun (pp. 181–182).
Experience: NPCs average 1D6+1 (2nd–7th level); player characters start at 1st level (`nightbane_core_night_prince_vampire` XP table) (pp. 181–182).

classAbilities.description: Powerful eagle-like flier — +5 strike and +5 dodge in the air (+2 strike/dodge on ground); +6 damage on swooping attacks. Ground speed 1D6×10+50 — metadata on race row (p. 169).

classAbilities.description: Claws 2D6 S.D.C.; bite 1D6 plus lethal poison (3D6 direct to H.P. first round, 1D6 per melee; save vs poison each melee; affects supernatural beings) — `race_snake_bird` innateBonuses.activation (p. 169).

classAbilities.name: Regeneration

classAbilities.name: Habitat & Alliances

classAbilities.name: Experience
classAbilities.description: Typical level 1D6+1 — Mystic/Snakebird XP table (`nightbane_core_snakebird_mystic`); a few reach near-legendary 12th+ levels (p. 169).

name: Nightlord R.C.C. Skills
description: Shadow O.C.C. for the Nightlord R.C.C. — Ba'al arch-villains (pp. 173–175; NPC only; not player characters). Occult Lore at 98%, all languages at 98%, and typically 10–20 additional skills at +20% from any category (centuries of experience). Hand to Hand: typically Martial Arts or Assassin. No conventional spell casting — matter/energy reality control on `race_nightlord`. Creates semi-independent Avatars (`race_nightlord_avatar`). Typical experience 2D4+5.
packageNotes: Shadow O.C.C. for `race_nightlord` (auto-mounted via forcedOccId). NPC stat block — campaign-ending Ba'al arch-villains; seldom encountered directly (pp. 173–174). Thoroughly corrupt — not player characters.
Alignment: any evil — most Diabolic (p. 175).
Core R.C.C. skills (p. 175): Occult Lore (`skill_lore_magic`) at 98%; knows all languages at 98% (many ancient/archaic tongues) — inherent fluency, not purchased from skill slots.

Additional skills: book lists 10–20 skills at +20% from any category reflecting centuries of experience. Catalog uses 10 related slots as the creation floor; GMs may assign up to 20 for powerful Nightlords at the table (p. 175).
No secondary skill picks in the book (p. 175).

Combat: typically Hand to Hand Martial Arts or Assassin — fixed natural-abilities combat, not purchased from related slots (`hth_martial_arts` default; Assassin equally typical via zero-cost upgrade path) (p. 175).
Disdains conventional spell casting — ample magic knowledge but P.P.E. fuels matter/energy reality control on `race_nightlord`, not spell catalog picks (pp. 173–175).

Creates semi-independent Physical and Astral Avatars (`race_nightlord_avatar`) — typically 2–3 max; destroying an Avatar costs the Nightlord all of that Avatar's S.D.C., H.P., and P.P.E. (heals 1/hour each) (pp. 173–174).

Supernatural abilities (regeneration, life-force vampirism, matter/energy control, dimensional senses, Mirrorwall, vulnerabilities) live on `race_nightlord` — see `classAbilities` cross-refs (pp. 174–175).

Commands at least 100 Hounds, 10 Hunters, 10 Hollow Men, and 5–10 Ashmedai — powerful Ba'al field ten to twenty times more; leadership often delegated to Night Princes (`race_night_prince`) and Avatars (pp. 175).
Typical starting experience: 2D4+5 (`nightbane_core_nightlord` XP table). Avatars typically half the creator's level (p. 175).

classAbilities.name: NPC Only (Ba'al Arch-Villain)
classAbilities.description: Campaign-ending threats — seldom encountered directly; even experienced Nightbane bands are hopelessly outmatched without layered defenses (pp. 173–174). Not player characters (p. 175).

classAbilities.name: Alignment
classAbilities.description: Any evil — most Diabolic. Good and neutral alignments excluded on `race_nightlord` demographics (p. 175).

classAbilities.name: Occult Lore
classAbilities.description: Lore: Magic (Occult Lore) at 98% — `occSkillsCore` `skill_lore_magic` with flat 98% baseline (p. 175).

classAbilities.name: All Languages at 98%
classAbilities.description: Inherent fluency in all languages at 98%, including many ancient and archaic tongues — not selected from skill slots. Assign specific language rows at the table only when a named tongue matters for checks (p. 175).

classAbilities.name: Centuries of Experience (+20%)
classAbilities.description: Typically 10–20 additional skills at +20% from any category — catalog provides 10 related slots; GMs may expand to 20 for individual Ba'al (p. 175).

classAbilities.name: Hand to Hand: Martial Arts or Assassin
classAbilities.description: Natural-abilities combat — typically Martial Arts or Assassin (book lists both as equally typical). `handToHandRules.defaultSkillId: hth_martial_arts`; zero-cost Assassin upgrade path (p. 175).

classAbilities.name: Create Avatars
classAbilities.description: Semi-independent Physical and Astral Avatars sharing the Nightlord's personality — war leaders, spies, and world-leader replacements. Typically 2–3 max (`race_nightlord_avatar`; metadata.maxAvatarsTypical) (pp. 173–174).

classAbilities.name: Avatar Destruction Backlash
classAbilities.description: When an Avatar is killed, the Nightlord immediately loses S.D.C., H.P., and P.P.E. equal to that Avatar's totals — recovers 1 point each per hour. Creating too many Avatars risks fatal backlash if all are destroyed (pp. 173–174).

classAbilities.name: Supernatural Regeneration & Invulnerability
classAbilities.description: Regenerate 20 S.D.C./H.P. at end of every melee round. Half damage from non-magical weapons and attacks; magic does full damage. See invisible through any masking; ley line/nexus sense 1 mile/level; nightvision 2000 ft — `race_nightlord` innateBonuses (pp. 174–175).

classAbilities.name: Unmasked Aura
classAbilities.description: At will, unmask supernatural aura — mortals save vs Horror Factor 18 (`unmaskedHorrorFactor`) (pp. 174–175).

classAbilities.name: Life Force Vampirism
classAbilities.description: Feed on helpless victims (not in combat) — 1 minute, up to 50 H.P./P.P.E. absorbed. Humans/Nightbane 1:1 P.P.E.; other minions 1 P.P.E. per 10 absorbed. Minimum 20 P.P.E./day or lose 20 from base daily — `race_nightlord` innateBonuses.activation (pp. 174–175).

classAbilities.name: Starvation & Half Capacity
classAbilities.description: At zero P.P.E., lose 1 P.E./day until dust (possible return if conditions right). Starved Ba'al function at half attacks, S.D.C., bonuses, and speed — metadata.starvedHalfCapacityThreshold (pp. 174–175).

classAbilities.name: Matter & Energy Control
classAbilities.description: P.P.E.-fueled reality shaping (2 attacks/action, line of sight, 1 mile): reshape/melt/transform inanimate matter; matter-energy meld armor/auras; absorb/disperse energy; release energy blasts (+3 strike); dimensional manipulation at spell costs; telekinesis at ×10 P.P.E. Living beings, organic matter, and magic artifacts immune — `race_nightlord` classAbilities (pp. 174–175).

classAbilities.name: Dimensional Senses
classAbilities.description: Sense Rifts, portals, teleport effects, and dimensional anomalies 5 miles/level — metadata.dimensionalSenseMilesPerLevel (p. 175).

classAbilities.name: Breach the Mirrorwall
classAbilities.description: Cross Mirrorwall: 10 P.P.E., 1 minute (not in combat). Send minions to Earth: 5 P.P.E. each, 1 minute — metadata.mirrorwallCrossPpe / minionSendToEarthPpeEach (pp. 174–175).

classAbilities.name: Dream Link
classAbilities.description: Linked to human thought/emotion; may send Astral Avatars into sensitive dreamers' minds to disturb, frighten, or occasionally recruit mortals (pp. 173–174).

classAbilities.name: Combat Bonuses
classAbilities.description: +3 initiative, +5 on all Perception rolls, +1 parry/dodge, +4 roll/pull punch, +6 save vs magic, +10 save vs horror factor — `race_nightlord` innateBonuses.modifiers and metadata. Supernatural strength damage (pp. 175).

classAbilities.name: Disdains Conventional Spellcasting
classAbilities.description: Ample magic knowledge — former sorcerers who prefer matter/energy control over spell casting. No spell catalog picks on this O.C.C. (pp. 173–175).

classAbilities.name: No Psionics
classAbilities.description: No psionic powers beyond natural abilities listed — `race_nightlord` psionics.capabilityType: none (p. 175).

classAbilities.name: Hit Points & P.P.E. Scaling
classAbilities.description: True H.P. = P.E. × level of experience. P.P.E. 2D6×100+1000 + 50 per level — `race_nightlord` vitals and metadata (pp. 175).

classAbilities.name: Vulnerabilities
classAbilities.description: Triple damage from Nightbane and Guardians; double from all other supernatural creatures (including vampires and Wampyrs) — applies to Nightlords and their Avatars (pp. 175).

classAbilities.name: Commanded Forces
classAbilities.description: At minimum: 100 Hounds (`race_hound`), 10 Hunters (`race_hunter`), 10 Hollow Men (`race_hollow_man`), 5–10 Ashmedai (`race_ashmedai`). Average Ba'al ten to twenty times more; most powerful ten to twenty above that. Leadership delegated to Night Princes (`race_night_prince`) and Avatars (pp. 175).

classAbilities.name: Optional Artifacts
classAbilities.description: Some Nightlords wield ancient artifacts and/or magic weapons and armor; most disdain weapons unless extremely powerful — GM-assigned only (p. 175).

classAbilities.name: Paired Avatar Race
classAbilities.description: Shadow construct row `race_nightlord_avatar` — Physical or Astral extensions at roughly half the Nightlord's typical level (pp. 173–175).

classAbilities.name: Experience
classAbilities.description: Typical level 2D4+5 — `nightbane_core_nightlord` XP table. Avatars typically half the creator's level (p. 175).

miscellaneous: Optional GM issue: ancient artifacts and/or magic weapons and armor — most Ba'al disdain conventional weapons unless extremely powerful (p. 175)

name: Night Prince R.C.C. Skills
description: Shadow O.C.C. for the Night Prince R.C.C. — Ba'al-Zebul lieutenants of the Nightlords (pp. 175–177; GM approval; player characters discouraged). Occult Lore (+20%), eight languages at 98%, ten related skills (+10%; prerequisites required) from any category, and twelve secondary skills. Hand to Hand: Assassin with +1 attack per melee at 1st level (`race_night_prince`). Natural illusion powers only (`ppeEngine`; no spell catalog picks). Typical experience 1D4+4.
packageNotes: Shadow O.C.C. for `race_night_prince` (auto-mounted via forcedOccId). Ba'al-Zebul — chief lieutenants of `race_nightlord`; rank below Avatars (`race_nightlord_avatar`) (pp. 175–176).
GM-approval R.C.C. — player characters discouraged but possible at G.M. discretion; twisted energy-vampire nature and terrible reputation make integration difficult (pp. 176–177).
Alignment: Diabolic or Miscreant — Aberrant and good/neutral excluded on race row. Renegade Night Princes rumored at G.M. discretion (pp. 176–177).
Night Squires: low-level, undistinguished Night Princes — same R.C.C. program at lower experience (p. 176).
Core R.C.C. skills (p. 177): Occult Lore (`skill_lore_magic`) at +20%; eight languages at 98% (great age) — eight `skill_language` core picks at flat 98% baseline.
Select 10 related skills from any category at +10% O.C.C. bonus. Prerequisites cannot be ignored — grey unavailable picks per Radical Visibility (p. 177).
Select 12 secondary skills at creation — no O.C.C. percentage bonus on secondary picks unless a specific skill grants otherwise (p. 177).

Combat: equivalent to Hand to Hand Assassin with +1 attack per melee from 1st level — fixed natural-abilities combat on `race_night_prince` (`hth_assassin` default here). Supernatural strength damage (pp. 176–177).

Magic: natural illusion constructs only — P.P.E. on `ppeEngine` and `race_night_prince` innateBonuses; may fake spellcasting with illusion effects. No conventional spell catalog picks; no psionic powers (pp. 176–177).
Illusion tier costs, range, volume, Horror Factor caps, and death-by-illusion rules live on `race_night_prince` — see `classAbilities` cross-refs (pp. 176–177).
Energy vampirism feeding rules on race row — minimum 5 P.P.E./day, satisfaction at 10–20/day (pp. 176–177).
P.P.E.: 1D4×100 at 1st level; +15 per level (`ppeEngine`) (p. 177).
Typical starting experience: 1D4+4 (`nightbane_core_night_prince_vampire` XP table — shared with vampire R.C.C.s) (p. 177).

classAbilities.name: GM Approval Optional R.C.C.
classAbilities.description: Ba'al-Zebul lieutenants — player characters possible at G.M. discretion but discouraged; energy-vampire nature and reputation make most factions hostile (pp. 176–177).

classAbilities.name: Ba'al-Zebul Role
classAbilities.description: Chief lieutenants of `race_nightlord` — human-looking illusion masters gifted to deceive mortals while other minions are illusion-immune. Ideal infiltrators and cult leaders (pp. 175–176).

classAbilities.name: Rank Below Avatars
classAbilities.description: Higher rank than all other minions except `race_nightlord_avatar` — war leaders and Nightlord delegates (pp. 175–176).

classAbilities.name: Earth Infiltration
classAbilities.description: Millennia on Earth — Cults of Eternal Night, Preserver Party manipulation, high-society masquerades (movie stars, corporate officers, political advisors) (pp. 175–176).

classAbilities.name: Renegades
classAbilities.description: Some betray overlords for power — cult control, mercenary work, or rival Nightlord alliances. Rumored anti-Nightlord defectors at G.M. discretion (p. 176).

classAbilities.name: Night Squires
classAbilities.description: Low-level, undistinguished Night Princes — same R.C.C. program at lower experience (p. 176).

classAbilities.name: Alignment
classAbilities.description: Diabolic or Miscreant only — Aberrant and good/neutral alignments excluded (`race_night_prince` demographics) (pp. 176–177).

classAbilities.name: Occult Lore (+20%)
classAbilities.description: Lore: Magic (Occult Lore) at +20% — `occSkillsCore` `skill_lore_magic` (p. 177).

classAbilities.name: Eight Languages at 98%
classAbilities.description: Eight core language picks at flat 98% due to great age — `occSkillsCore` language vouchers; assign 98% baseline at the table (schema choice vouchers do not carry `basePercent`) (p. 177).

classAbilities.name: Elective R.C.C. Skill Package
classAbilities.description: Ten related skills from any category (+10% O.C.C. bonus) and twelve secondary skills — no O.C.C. % on secondary picks (p. 177).

classAbilities.name: Prerequisites Required
classAbilities.description: Related-skill prerequisites cannot be waived — unavailable picks greyed per Radical Visibility (p. 177).

classAbilities.name: Hand to Hand: Assassin (+1 APM)
classAbilities.description: Combat equivalent to Hand to Hand Assassin with one additional strike/attack per melee from 1st level — `handToHandRules.defaultSkillId` + `race_night_prince` innateBonuses.modifiers.apm (pp. 176–177).

classAbilities.name: Supernatural Endurance
classAbilities.description: Heal 1D4×10 S.D.C./H.P. every minute. See invisible and all illusions (including other Night Princes'). Impervious to mind control. Nightvision 500 ft; ley line/nexus sense 1 mile. No food, water, or air required — `race_night_prince` classAbilities (pp. 176–177).

classAbilities.name: Energy Vampirism
classAbilities.description: Minimum 5 H.P./P.P.E. per day to survive; 10–20/day for satisfaction. Miss a meal: −1 P.P.E. (restore with +5 extra on next feeding). At 0 P.P.E., lose 1 P.E./day until dust — innateBonuses.activation metadata (pp. 176–177).

classAbilities.name: Illusionary Powers
classAbilities.description: P.P.E.-fueled constructs — range 100 ft + 20 ft/level; volume 10 cu ft/level. Tier costs per minute: 2 (sound/minor visual), 5 (sound+image or smell), 10 (touch/temperature), 20 (full sensory), 60 (panorama). Resisted by save vs magic when victim suspects; Difficult Perception or worse for multi-sense illusions — `race_night_prince` metadata (pp. 176–177).

classAbilities.name: Illusion Horror Factor
classAbilities.description: Base H.F. 10 on illusions; +1 per 3 additional P.P.E. spent (beyond illusion cost); maximum H.F. 18 — metadata.illusionHorrorFactorMax (pp. 176–177).

classAbilities.name: Death by Illusion
classAbilities.description: Victims who believe illusionary harm can suffer real damage or death from shock — metadata.deathByIllusionRules (pp. 176–177).

classAbilities.name: Fake Spellcasting
classAbilities.description: May replicate spell-like effects using natural illusion constructs — not conventional spell learning (pp. 176–177).

classAbilities.name: No Conventional Spellcasting
classAbilities.description: No spell catalog picks — illusion constructs only via `ppeEngine` P.P.E. pool (pp. 176–177).

classAbilities.name: No Psionics
classAbilities.description: No psionic powers — `race_night_prince` psionics.capabilityType: none; Psychic category forbidden (pp. 176–177).

classAbilities.name: Perception Bonus
classAbilities.description: +5 on all Perception rolls — `race_night_prince` metadata.perceptionBonus (p. 176).

classAbilities.name: Hit Points & P.P.E. Scaling
classAbilities.description: True H.P. = P.E.×2 + 2D6 per level. P.P.E. 1D4×100 + 15 per level — `ppeEngine` and `race_night_prince` vitals (p. 177).

classAbilities.name: Vulnerabilities
classAbilities.description: Double damage from Nightbane attacks/Talents and Guardian energy powers — `race_night_prince` classAbilities (p. 177).

classAbilities.name: Commanded Forces & Alliances
classAbilities.description: Often command Hound Masters (`race_hound_master`), Hollow Men (`race_hollow_man`), and Ashmedai (`race_ashmedai`); recruit Priests of Night (`race_priest_of_night`). May lead full Nightlord armies — metadata.commandedMinionRaceIds (pp. 175–176).

classAbilities.name: Experience
classAbilities.description: Typical level 1D4+4 — Night Prince & Vampire XP table (`nightbane_core_night_prince_vampire`) (p. 177).

miscellaneous: Typical infiltrator cover: high-society wardrobe, credentials, and props matching jet-setter, corporate, or political-advisor masquerades (pp. 175–176)
Optional GM issue: cult-leader regalia, Preserver Party network contacts, or war-leader field kit
